word: H5993 - עַמִּי נָדִיב - ʻAmmîy Nâdîyb - am-mee' naw-deeb' - Amminadib

sections.paragraph:
Original: עמּי נדיב

Transliteration: ‛ammı̂y nâdı̂yb

Phonetic: am-mee' naw-deeb'

BDB Definition:

Amminadib = "my people are willing"
  1. an unknown person noted for the swiftness of his chariots
    1. possibly not a person but should be translated 'my willing people'

Origin: from H5971 and H5081

TWOT entry: None

Part(s) of speech: Proper Name Masculine
